space_scope: Europe from Bronze Age (-3000) through the end of WWII (1945). Includes Mediterranean (Greece, Rome, Carthage, Phoenicia), Iberian, Italian, French/Frankish, German/HRE, British Isles, Nordic, Slavic, Balkan, Russian, and Ottoman-in-Europe polities. Mediterranean/Levant/North-African neighbors are referenced where they intersect European history (Carthage, Byzantine East, Crusader states, Ottoman expansion). Post-1945 Cold War and EU states are out of scope.

field_notes.macro_period: Large chronological period (Bronze Age, Iron Age, Classical, Migration Period, Early Medieval, High Medieval, Late Medieval, Early Modern, Long 19th, World Wars). Not China-specific.
field_notes.dynasty_name: Dynasty, ruling house, civilization phase, or republic/constitutional regime label. For city-states and republics use the constitutional regime (e.g. 'Republican Rome', 'Venetian Republic', 'Florentine Republic'); for empires use the dynasty/house name (e.g. 'Julio-Claudian', 'Capetian', 'Hohenzollern', 'Habsburg-Lorraine').
field_notes.modern_admin_units_raw: Modern country and first-level administrative approximation; not a claim of exact historical borders. Use pipe-separated ISO3 country codes (FRA|DEU|ITA) and optional ADM1 codes for federations (DE-BY|IT-25|ES-CT).
field_notes.ruler_temple_name: East Asian style field; leave empty unless a culture has an equivalent formal name. European rulers typically use ruler_name (regnal name) and ruler_personal_name (birth name).
field_notes.v02_*: Retained only for v03 compatibility; empty for non-v02 datasets.
field_notes.historical_contexts: Periodization metadata: which macro_period a given year falls under, large transitional events (fall of Rome, Reformation, French Revolution, etc.). Provides UI hints when the year scrubber crosses major boundaries.
field_notes.ruler_continuity: European succession rules vary widely (primogeniture, elective monarchy, papal election, republican magistracy). Use the polity's documented system when assigning reign years; for elective monarchies (HRE, Polish-Lithuanian), the gap between predecessor's death and successor's election may leave a year with row_granularity=year_polity_unmatched_ruler.
